news.t: This Semiconductor ETF Can Continue Sizzling
Semiconductor stocks look relatively weak as S&P 500 attempts record, chart shows
SOXQ: This Low-Fee Semiconductor ETF Is Overlooked
Exposure to Russia Not Seen as a Material Headwind for Chip Stocks

holdings.infoBox: SOXQ is an equity ETF with a total of 32 individual holdings. The top holdings are Micron Technology stock at 11.48%, NVIDIA at 8.38%, Marvell Technology at 8.26%, Broadcom at 7.21%, and Intel at 6.59%.

overview.description: The Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the PHLX / Semiconductor index, a modified market-cap-weighted index of 30 US-listed semiconductor companies. SOXQ was launched on Jun 11, 2021 and is issued by Invesco.

data.title: This Semiconductor ETF Can Continue Sizzling
data.text: It's not just Nvidia (NVDA). Other semiconductor stocks, including Broadcom (AVGO), are surging this year, as the artificial intelligence (AI) build out continues in earnest.

data.title: Semiconductor ETFs: The Trade Broadens
data.text: Several years ago, semiconductor ETFs were often viewed as a Nvidia (NVDA) story. With Nvidia driving much of the group's growth and performance, some investors questioned the need for broader exposur...

data.title: Semiconductor stocks look relatively weak as S&P 500 attempts record, chart shows
data.text: Semiconductor stocks are rising this month, but the sector is relatively weak compared to the S&P 500 despite Nvidia's recent surge, according to Bespoke Investment Group.

data.title: SOXQ: This Low-Fee Semiconductor ETF Is Overlooked
data.text: Semiconductor stocks have unquestionably been the hottest part of the market, and the Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (NASDAQ:SOXQ) is an excellent way for investors to play the semiconductor space. I’...

data.title: AI is a wonderful sector to be in, says Matt Bryson of Wedbush Securities
data.text: Matt Bryson of Wedbush Securities joins 'TechCheck' to discuss the swelling supply of semiconductors, bullish picks for the chip sector and the outlook for chip providers in gaming.

data.title: Exposure to Russia Not Seen as a Material Headwind for Chip Stocks
data.text: Russia is a 'de minimis direct purchaser of semiconductors,' say analysts at Bernstein.

data.title: Is the worst of the semiconductor shortage behind us?
